steps.text: Provide operational oversight and advice to the Assistant Secretary, program directors, and regional administrators on compliance with this instruction and related processes.
Manage the OSHA directive system, including dissemination of policy and non-policy issuances.
Oversee the administration of the program and provide training in its operation.
Oversee program administration and provide operational training.
As the Agency Administrative Officer, signs all policy, procedural issuances, and national directives pertaining to administrative matters.
Effectively implement and comply with provisions of this and related instructions.
Assess and routinely monitor program performance compliance.
Responsible for the review and assessment of their active directives on an annual basis with a final document of the review findings at the end of each fiscal year.
Initiate and develop proposed NPIs within the scope of their authority or significantly affecting the operations of their programs.

Based on the subject and intended application of a proposed NPI, request review and clearance by appropriate directorates, regional offices, Office of the Solicitor (SOL), the Assistant Secretary, etc.
Provide proposed NPI and necessary background information to ensure the proposed issuance is thoroughly understood and considered.
Establish a due date for response return, allowing no more than three weeks turnaround time.
Review and consider comments received and address them as appropriate.

Refer to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary any unsolved disagreements or issues concerning the substance or potential policy implications of the proposed NPI or to the priority accorded to the proposed NPI’s review that emerged during the clearance process.
Ensure that systems for receiving, reviewing, tracking, concurring.
Ensure that systems for receiving, reviewing, tracking, concurring, and issuing NPIs are effective for consistently complying with requirements of this instruction.

Consider each proposed NPI and promptly respond to the originator regarding technical considerations, potential impact, implications of Agency-wide policy and operations, and anticipated reception by Agency customers.
Initiate and develop proposed issuance of the new or revised policy and procedures.
Ensure reviewers receive the appropriate background information.
Establish a due date for response return, allowing no less than two weeks turnaround time.
Obtain guidance and clearance from the Office of the Solicitor.
Obtain input and policy clearance of proposed issuances/directives from each program director and others identified as mandatory clearance offices.
Refer to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for any unsolved disagreements or issues concerning the proposed policy issuance.
Consider each proposed issuance and respond accordingly.
Program Director and Regional Administrator receiving a policy issuance for review and clearance
Directives Officer administer the OSHA directive program.
Ensure compliance with directives and related instructions.
Provide advice, assistance, and training to national office directorates and regional offices in directives management.
Provide new or revised directive documents to the web administrator for posting to the directives webpage.
Periodically notify the web administrator of updates to the webpage based on cancellations and expired directives.
Director initiating national directives provide unsigned 508 compliant PDF file versions to directives officer for posting.
Update necessary directorate webpages to reflect new directives.
Disseminate directives as appropriate.
Regional Administrator review and respond to draft directives received from national office directorates.
Implement OSHA national directives throughout the region.
Clear and publish regional directives supplementing OSHA national directives.
Provide 508-compliant PDF file versions to the directives officer for posting.
Update necessary regional webpages to reflect new directives.
Disseminate regional instructions as necessary.
Area Director distribute new and updated directives to all employees.
Web Administrator maintain the official directives page on the OSHA website.
Review directives received from the directives officer to ensure proper 508 compliance.
Post new or revised directives to the directives webpage.
Develop and publish written procedures that define and specify mandatory clearance processes and requirements.
Specify formatting requirements for material type.
Establish clearance timeframes.
Establish the process for comment resolution.
Identify all mandatory review and clearance officials (intra- and extra-organizational).
Establish means to ensure review and clearance officials receive copies of appropriate policy and other background information to ensure the proposed issuance is understood.

Establish means to ensure the views of subordinate staff, mandatory review and clearance officials, and the Office of the Solicitor, as necessary are obtained and considered in the clearance and revision process.
Specify the official authorized to approve the release of the NPI.
Specify the official responsible for maintaining records documenting the views and clearance of each clearance official.
Specify the official responsible for maintaining and updating these written procedures.
Communicate frequently with Agency offices and stakeholders to agree on policies, procedures, and required actions.
Informally circulate drafts, reports, and other materials related to a directive drafting before conducting the formal clearance process.
Consult with DEP and DCSP for federal program change language during the informal clearance.
Ensure that the appropriate language is circulated for review during the formal clearance process.
Review construction-related directives by DOC.
Elevate disputes with clearance officials to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Notify affected parties when the approved directive is posted on the directives webpage as necessary.
Review the draft directive.
Request an extension from the originator as soon as possible if necessary.
Complete item number 9 and 10 of the OSHA 201.
Return the OSHA 201 with comments to the originator.
Send concurrence or comments electronically (e.g., email, shared folders, SharePoint, etc.).
Negotiate the acceptance of comments with the originator or originating Program Director.
Present the case to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Review the directive clearance packet to determine if complete and accurate for approval clearance.
Provide revisions request from the Office of the Assistant Secretary (OAS) to the originator for necessary updates.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator.
Request the directive be formatted to a 508-compliant PDF file.
Coordinate union review through the Labor Management Relations Officer.
Assign an effective date based on the approval by the union, delayed posting request, or pending press release.
Provide the final 508-compliant PDF file directive to the web administrator for posting to the Directives webpage.
Review 508-compliant PDF file for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF file to OSHA Directives webpage within the requested timeframe.
Notify the directives officer of availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Convene a meeting with directorates and regional offices to provide information on the content of the direction.
Provide a copy of the proposed direction to each clearance office required to provide clearance if the direction was issued as an instruction.
Consider the proposed direction and provide comments within five working days.
Elevate outstanding issue(s) to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary, and as necessary, to the Assistant Secretary for a decision regarding the issue of contention.
Elevate outstanding issue(s) to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary.
Prepare the direction clearance packet.
Submit the clearance packet to the directives officer for processing.
Provide a 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer.
Coordinate the publication of the direction with the web administrator.
Review the direction six months after issuance.
Review non-substantive changes with the Program Director or Regional Administrator.
Review and approve non-significant changes with the Program Director and other relevant directorates.
Continue normal clearance process for significant changes.
Implement and comply with the provisions of this instruction and with related instructions governing the management of policy and non-policy issues.
Self-assess and routinely monitor the directive process using the checklist on the directives tools webpage to ensure compliance with the provisions of the instruction.
Responsible for the review and assessment of their active directives on an annual basis with a final document of the review findings maintained in the national office or regional files.
Conduct a full assessment of the files for the directives approved that year.
Ensure that all appropriate documents are included in the file.
Send an email or memo to the Director, Office of Audit, DAP, or designee documenting the completion of the requirement no later than September 30 of the fiscal year.
Retain completion documentation in the directive file record.
Confirm the self-assessment documentation was received within the established deadline.
Include the auditing of the assessment process as part of its audit responsibilities.
Ensure that the directives process is part of its regular auditing tasks.
